---
title: "Quarto + Julia — Ship of Tools preview fixture"
author: "Ship of Tools"
format: html
---

## What this is

A Quarto (`.qmd`) fixture for Ship of Tools's preview pipeline. In the file
navigator it renders **as markdown in-pane** — this prose, the headings,
the fenced chunks, and the math below — with the YAML header above
skipped. Press **`o`** to render the full document *without* running code
(fast) in the browser, or **`O`** to execute the Julia chunks and open the
computed result.

## Prose, math, and a list

Inline math like $f(x) = \sin(x) + \cos(x)$ renders, and so does a display
block:

$$
\int_0^{\pi} \sin(x)\,\mathrm{d}x = 2.
$$

- Quarto markdown is a superset of CommonMark.
- Code chunks are `` ```{julia} `` fences with `#|` cell options.
- `o` shows the chunks as source; `O` runs them and embeds the output.

## Executable Julia (base only — runs under `O`)

These use only base Julia, so `O` executes cleanly with just the Quarto
`julia` engine — no extra packages required.

```{julia}
#| echo: true
# Sum of squares 1..10
xs = 1:10
squares = [x^2 for x in xs]
total = sum(squares)
println("sum of squares 1..10 = ", total)
total
```

```{julia}
#| echo: true
f(x) = sin(x) + cos(x)
[round(f(x); digits = 3) for x in range(0, 2π; length = 5)]
```

## A plot (CairoMakie — Ship of Tools's plotting convention)

Shown for reference and marked `eval: false`, so the fixture still renders
under `O` even when CairoMakie isn't installed in the Quarto Julia env.
Remove the `eval: false` option to actually produce the figure.

```{julia}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
using CairoMakie
fig = Figure()
ax = Axis(fig[1, 1]; title = "sin + cos", xlabel = "x", ylabel = "f(x)")
xs = range(0, 2π; length = 200)
lines!(ax, xs, sin.(xs) .+ cos.(xs))
fig
```
